base_case: Bull (melt-up intact) — 5th consecutive ATH close, but decelerating; 30–60 day horizon favors continued grind toward Wave 1 trim trigger at 7,696 before any meaningful pullback.
trend_regime: Golden Cross intact: 50-day above 200-day, price above both. +10.5% premium to 50-day SMA and +15.0% premium to 200-day SMA — both at cycle highs (extended).
valuation_bucket: 20–22x Forward P/E (21.73x) — Cautious posture; only 96 pts (1.3%) below the 22x Wave 1 trim trigger at 7,696.

signals.bollinger_position: pulled slightly back inside upper rail after riding upper band 12 sessions

what_changed_today: No prior sessions on record in memory; this is the baseline session.
5th consecutive ATH close, but daily gain decelerated to +0.26% (+19.90 pts) — smallest of the 5-session streak.
F&G fell to 59 (3rd consecutive decline at new ATH: 61 -> 60 -> 59) — textbook sentiment divergence.
VIX dropped to ~15, the lowest print of the entire May-June rally — peak complacency.
McClellan Breadth ticked up marginally from ~1,000 to ~1,020 — first microscopic breadth improvement in 7+ sessions.
Wave 1 trim trigger now only 96 pts (1.3%) away; intraday high 7,617 reached 21.78x forward P/E.
narrative_summary: SPX printed its 5th consecutive all-time closing high at 7,599.96 but the +0.26% gain was the smallest of the streak, with a gap-down open, a weak close versus the 7,617 intraday high, and four hours of churn near round-number resistance — a market visibly running low on buyers. Every technical and sentiment gauge sits at a cycle extreme: forward P/E 21.73x just 96 pts below the 22x Wave 1 trim trigger (7,696), ERP pinned near +0.10% for a 7th session, VIX at a rally-low ~15, and Put/Call at ~0.60, all against a persistent backdrop of junk-bond Extreme Fear and weak breadth/price-strength internals. Monte Carlo still strongly favors the melt-up (79.6% Wave 1 first, median ~4 sessions to 7,700), so the disciplined stance is to HOLD with a live GTC trim at 7,696 and emphatically not add here. The single most important read is the sentiment divergence — the crowd is incrementally less excited at incrementally higher prices, the hallmark of a fully-invested market.
open_questions: Does McClellan Breadth cross 1,100 mid-week (weakens pullback thesis) or roll back below 1,000 (intensifies trap-door risk)?
Will SPX reach 7,696 via clean grind or gap up through 7,650, triggering a manual Wave 1 execution?
How long can ERP near +0.10% and 7 sessions of junk-bond Extreme Fear coexist with new ATHs before credit divergence resolves?
Does the intraday RSI collapse (80 -> mid-40s -> ~75) signal early institutional distribution at the open?

decision_matrix.valuation: Forward P/E 21.73x, ERP ~+0.10% (7th session near zero) — Caution/near ceiling; trim bias as Wave 1 (7,696) approaches.
decision_matrix.technicals: Trend bullish (50d>200d) but extended +10.5%/+15.0%; RSI ~75, MFI overbought, Bollinger pulled inside upper rail after 12 sessions riding it — overbought/exhaustion.
decision_matrix.sentiment: F&G 59 (Greed, 3rd decline at ATH), Put/Call ~0.60 complacent, VIX ~15 lowest of rally — complacency peak with bearish divergence.
decision_matrix.risk: Leverage/forced-selling risk low by VIX (<20) but complacency-reversal risk elevated; junk-bond Extreme Fear and weak breadth are latent systemic flags.

confirming_evidence: Trend regime confirmed bullish: 50-day above 200-day, price above both, 5 consecutive ATH closes.
Monte Carlo strongly favors melt-up: 79.6% P(Wave 1 7,696 first) vs 20.1% downside; 86.3% P(Wave 1 hit within 60d); median ~4 sessions to 7,700.
VIX at ~15 (<20) keeps moving averages reliable and forced-selling risk low — full framework active.
MFI grinding higher and back in overbought confirms Monday's moderate up-volume was relatively clean.

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: Price makes 5th consecutive new ATH — momentum and trend intact.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: F&G declines for 3rd straight session (61->60->59) at higher prices — crowd incrementally less excited; hallmark of a fully-invested market.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: Sentiment divergence at new highs is a trim-confirmation context; never act on sentiment alone — confirm with technical levels.
chart_refs: CNN Fear & Greed component table (June 1 vs May 28)

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: Equity at all-time highs with VIX collapsing to 15 signals risk-on confidence.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: Junk Bond Demand at EXTREME FEAR (spread ~1.33%) for a 7th consecutive session while equity rallies — credit market refuses to confirm the melt-up.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: Spreads holding stress while price rallies is a credit-market warning — trim bias / reduce aggression.

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: Index prints new ATHs and McClellan ticked up to ~1,020 — first marginal breadth improvement in 7+ sessions.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: Breadth (McClellan ~1,020) and Stock Price Strength (~2%, 7th session) remain in FEAR while the index recovers — narrow leadership.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: Breadth weakening/lagging while the index makes new highs raises pullback risk; watch for confirmation above 1,100 before discounting.

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: Primary first-hit P(Wave 1 7,696 first) is 79.6% — well above the 65% actionable threshold for the melt-up path.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: Secondary test P(7,700 first vs 7,400) is 64.3% — just below the 65% threshold, a caution flag against adding; valuation/ERP argue against deployment.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: Monte Carlo edge < 65% = insufficient; not a green light to add. Combined with ERP ~0.10%, do not add — hold only.

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: RSI rebounded to ~75 into the close — buyers stepped back in.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: RSI collapsed intraday from ~80 to mid-40s on an aggressive gap-down open — possible early institutional distribution; close was weak vs 7,617 intraday high.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: Bearish intraday momentum swings near resistance support trim-readiness, not adds.

primary_tension: A strong Monte Carlo melt-up edge (79.6% to Wave 1 first, trim trigger 7,696 only 96 pts away) versus a stack of cycle-extreme exhaustion and divergence signals — F&G falling at new ATHs, junk-bond Extreme Fear, weak breadth, ERP near zero, and VIX-15 complacency — which together argue the rally is running out of buyers.

monte_carlo.conditional_cascade: P(Wave 1 7,696 hit within 60d) = 86.3%; P(7,700 hit within 60d) = 85.9%; secondary P(7,700 first vs 7,400) = 64.3% (just below threshold); P(38.2% Fib 7,149 hit within 60d) = 43.2%.
